proves: Out-of-process grader with randomised inputs: correct general solutions score 1.0; the in-process exit-code hijack and monkeypatch score 0.0 (verdict process never imports the candidate); and answer-hardcoding scores 0.0 (fixed/precomputed outputs cannot match the trusted reference computed on freshly randomised data). Remaining surface: a candidate that fully reimplements the reference logic is a correct solution, not a hack.
